summary: pyOpenMS-Viz is a Python library that provides a simple interface for extending the plotting capabilities of Pandas DataFrames for creating static or interactive visualizations of mass spectrometry data.
pyOpenMS-Viz integrates seamlessly with various plotting library backends including matplotlib, bokeh, and plotly.

Chromatograms can be plotted using kind = chromatogram with retention time on the x-axis and intensity on the y-axis. Peak Maps can be plotted using kind = peakmap with mass-to-charge on the x-axis, retention time on the y-axis, and intensi
3D plotting is enabled using plot_3d=True parameter in Peak Maps.
3D plotting for Peak Maps is currently only supported for ms_matplotlib and ms_plotly backends.
pyOpenMS-viz plotting occurs by calling the .plot() method on a pandas dataframe.
Mandatory plotting values for pyOpenMS-viz include column names for the x and y axes and the kind of plot.
PLOTLY backend is available for mass spectrometry plots including PLOTLYChromatogramPlot, PLOTLYMobilogramPlot, PLOTLYSpectrumPlot, and PLOTLYPeakMapPlot.
